title: Request Smuggling, Response Splitting, Parser Differentials, and Downgrades · Go Semantics Case: Target Decoding Differential
summary: A front end and origin disagree on length, whitespace, duplicate fields, target decoding, and line endings; the team tests each parser alone and forwards ambiguous messages after normalization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the target decoding differential increment in multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.
Reject or consistently handle bare line endings, whitespace before colons, obsolete folding, leading blank lines, and invalid request-line separators across every hop.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Target Decoding Differential · Theory Target Decoding Differential
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2815 in a partner TLS endpoint negotiating HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 through ALPN. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.
